data.note: Counts confirmed stable as of SM self-audit 2026-03-10. Last verification batch was 2026-03-05. No new verification runs since then. skill_type counts corrected from 7027/4071 to 5692/5406 after VerifiedSkill schema fix (2026-03-05).

sample_patterns.reason: FP: 8x markdown_injection in sweagent browser/viewer tools constructing data: URIs + protobuf binary
FP: all injection hits in test files and nostr bus protocol
FP: 14x unicode_escape_seq/fromCharCode in minified frontend bundles (KaTeX, mermaid, DataTables)
FP: 27x unicode_escape_seq = Chinese text labels in eval script

FP: regex_ignore_previous in SemanticValidator detection logic

entries.title: Current recommendations
data: TAG_DEPTH: 8857/11098 skills have shallow tags (depth 0-1). Run auto_tag enrichment.
VERIFY_COVERAGE: 6623/11098 skills still unverified (40.3% covered). Run next batch. Priority: --type agent A-tier.
A_TIER_AGENT_GAP: Only ~1% agent A-tier verified (4/394). #1 next coverage priority. Use --type agent --limit 100 batches.

CRAWL_STALENESS: 4/6 hubs are >7 days stale (mcp_so/glama/github_search: 13d, claude_skills_hub: 11d). skills_sh still pending (no runner). Only skillsmp is fresh (6d). PM should consider triggering crawl refresh.
OWNERSHIP_GAP: 9 scripts lack explicit AGENTS.md ownership — report sent to PM for assignment (see SM self-audit 2026-03-10).

entries.title: Agent skill catalog aligned with MCP structure
entries.description: 4,801 agent skills had installs hidden in tags (installs:N) with stars=0. Extracted installs into proper field. SM selection now uses priority_score() = max(stars, installs) for unified MCP+agent ranking. High-install agents (243K extract-errors) now properly prioritized.

entries.title: Dual catalog structure: MCP vs Agent skill gaps
entries.description: CORRECTED 2026-03-05: MCP servers: 5,692 (not 6,297 — schema was stripping skill_type). Agent skills: 5,406 (not 4,801). The VerifiedSkill Pydantic schema was missing skill_type and installs fields, causing model_dump() to drop them and default to mcp_server. Fixed by adding both fields to schema. Build pipeline now correctly classifies skills. Tier 1 now has 677 agent skills (was 0 before fix). Unified priority scoring: max(stars, installs) used everywhere.

entries.title: SM --type filter: separate MCP/agent catalog selection
entries.description: sm_select_targets.py now supports --type mcp|agent flag for separate catalog selection. Batch 11 (first agent-only batch): 100 agent skills verified, 99 pass + 1 PM override (supabase monorepo). All S-tier installs selected correctly. Display now shows separate MCP/Agent tier breakdowns. Agent verification coverage: ~16% (up from 14%).

entries.title: S-tier completion milestone
entries.description: S-tier is 100% verified as of 2026-03-05. Total: 193/193 (35 MCP + 158 agent). All high-install agent skills (S-tier: 100K+ installs) and high-star MCP S-tier skills are fully covered. This is the first tier to reach 100% coverage. Next focus: A-tier.

entries.title: A-tier agent coverage gap — #1 next priority
entries.description: Agent A-tier (10K–100K installs) has only ~1% coverage: 4/394 verified. This is the largest high-value gap in the catalog. MCP A-tier coverage is significantly higher. SM should direct next batches at --type agent A-tier skills until meaningful coverage is achieved (target: 50%+). Use: SM_TARGETS=$(python3 scripts/review/sm_select_targets.py --type agent --limit 100 --output-ids)

data.next_action: Run --type agent batches targeting A-tier until 50%+ coverage
